summary: Frontend Translation Testing - Hebrew to English. Most UI elements successfully translated to English. However, Hebrew text remains in API responses from backend. The frontend components correctly display data but the backend returns Hebrew values in `_he` suffixed fields (dominant_direction_he, field_narrative_he, action_he, etc.). User-generated content in database remains in Hebrew. RTL text direction issues found with question marks appearing at beginning of sentences.

critical.issue: GLOBE_DIR_LABELS contains Hebrew text: {'recovery': 'התאוששות', 'order': 'סדר', 'contribution': 'תרומה', 'exploration': 'חקירה'}. Should be English labels.
Returns Hebrew fields: dominant_direction_he, field_narrative_he, momentum_he, ai_field_interpretation
Returns Hebrew field: dominant_direction_he used in FieldImpactLayer

ui_bugs.issue: Uses field.field_narrative_he and compass.dominant_direction_he - displays Hebrew from API
Displays dominantHe + ' Leading' - shows Hebrew direction from API

integration_issues.issue: Hebrew text 'התאוששות Leading' displayed from API response
Action descriptions (action_he, direction_he, meanings) stored and displayed in Hebrew from database

issues: Question mark at beginning of sentences (RTL residue): '?What center are you operating from today' and '?How does your action impact the world'

onboarding_modal: Answer one question. See your trust state.
daily_base_selection: What center are you operating from today
Select a daily base to continue
field_impact: How does your action impact the world
Every action you take is added to the global human field
The field is the sum of all choices — yours and others
Go to the system to see the full globe

locations: Action record titles: חקירה, תרומה, סדר
Action descriptions: כתבתי רשימת יעדים, ארגנתי את סביבת העבודה, etc.
profile_page.source: Database stores user actions and AI interpretations in Hebrew

action_items: CRITICAL: Update /app/backend/constants.py - Change GLOBE_DIR_LABELS to English: {'recovery': 'Recovery', 'order': 'Order', 'contribution': 'Contribution', 'exploration': 'Exploration'}
Fix RTL text direction - Question marks should be at end of sentences, not beginning
Review all _he suffix fields in backend routes and either: (a) create English equivalents or (b) update frontend to use English direction keys with client-side mapping
Consider: Profile action records and AI interpretations may need translation migration for existing Hebrew data
critical_code_review_comments: EntryLayer.js lines 37-41: Uses _he fields from API - should use English versions
FieldImpactLayer.js line 72: Displays dominantHe directly - should use directionLabels[dominant] instead
/app/backend/constants.py: GLOBE_DIR_LABELS has Hebrew values that propagate through all APIs
Profile action records contain Hebrew from database - historical data migration may be needed

success_rate.frontend_static_ui: 95% - All static UI text translated to English
success_rate.frontend_dynamic_data: 50% - API data still returns Hebrew in _he fields
success_rate.profile_page: 60% - Static labels in English, dynamic data in Hebrew

context_for_next_testing_agent: Hebrew-to-English translation partially complete. Static UI text is in English. Hebrew remains in: (1) GLOBE_DIR_LABELS in constants.py returning Hebrew direction labels, (2) field_narrative_he and other _he fields from APIs, (3) user action records stored in Hebrew in database. The frontend components EntryLayer.js and FieldImpactLayer.js use these _he fields directly. Fix needed in backend constants.py and frontend components to use English direction mapping. RTL question mark positioning issue exists in DailyBaseSelection and FieldImpactLayer.

mocked_apis.note: All APIs use real database operations
